quoteTime: about eight o’clock in the morning

quoteFirst: of this Battery, they set busily about repairing some Part of it, and on the 20th had built up some Embrazures and mounted two Guns, and fired them on the Bomb-Battery again, which the Admiral observing, ordered the _Rippon_, a sixty Gun ship, to go and anchor as near it, as possible, and keep firing on it to prevent the Enemy's working on it any farther[_L_], so that all the next Day the Army was in a State of Tranquillity, and on _Sunday_ the 22d their grand Battery of twenty Guns being finished,
quoteTime: about eight o'Clock in the Morning
quoteLast: began to play very briskly on the Castle, as did the Bomb-Battery, and thirty or forty Cohorns and Royals planted on the Platform behind the Cannon[_M_], which the Enemy returned as briskly from the Castle, the four Ships[_N_] (Don _Blass_'s in particular) _St. Joseph_'s, and some few Guns from the _Barradera_, so that the Work was warm on both Sides.
title: An Account of the expedition to Carthagena, with explanatory notes and observations
